xPosts.content: I often tell my students not to be misled by the name 'artificial intelligence' — there is nothing artificial about it. A.I. is made by humans, intended to be used by humans, and impacts humans.
xPosts.highlights: Fei-Fei Li emphasizes that AI is fundamentally human-centric—created by humans, for humans, and affecting human society, challenging the notion of it being purely 'artificial'.
xPosts.worthReading: It provides a foundational philosophical perspective on AI's human origins and societal impact from a leading researcher.
